# TinyUa

> A lightweight, from-scratch OPC UA client stack for .NET 8. Core libraries have zero dependency on the OPC Foundation SDK.

## Intro

TinyUa implements the full OPC UA binary protocol from scratch — binary encoding, security policies (4 policies + 3 token types), transport layer with message chunking, and a high-level client with Fluent API. It targets .NET 8 and uses only `System.Security.Cryptography` for all cryptographic operations. The OPC Foundation SDK (`OPCFoundation.NetStandard.Opc.Ua`) is only used in test and example projects for reference comparisons.

## Quick Start

```bash
dotnet build TinyUa.sln
dotnet run --project TinyUa.Example -- selftest
```

Minimal code:
```csharp
using TinyUa.Core.Client;

await using var client = await UaClient
    .ConnectTo("opc.tcp://server:4840")
    .WithAppName("MyApp")
    .WithSecurity("Basic256Sha256")
    .WithUserName("user", "pass")
    .BuildAndRunAsync();

// Simple typed read
var time = await client.ReadValueAsync<DateTime>("i=2258");
Console.WriteLine($"ServerTime: {time:HH:mm:ss.fff}");

// Simple write
var result = await client.WriteAsync("ns=2;s=SetPoint", 42.0);
Console.WriteLine(result.StatusCode.IsGood ? "OK" : result.StatusCode.GetStatusText());
```

## API

### Connection & Configuration

**Fluent Builder** (`UaClient.ConnectTo(url)`):
- `.WithSecurity("Basic256Sha256")` — set security policy (None / Basic256Sha256 / Aes128Sha256RsaOaep / Aes256Sha256RsaPss)
- `.WithUserName(user, pass)` — username/password authentication (password is RSA-OAEP encrypted)
- `.WithAppName("MyApp")` — set application name
- `.WithRequestTimeout(5000)` — per-step network timeout in ms (default 30000)
- `.WithSessionTimeout(ms)` — session lifetime (default 3600000)
- `.WithReconnectRetries(max)` — max reconnect attempts (default -1 = infinite)
- `.WithoutReconnect()` — disable auto-reconnect
- `.WithErrorMode(ErrorMode.ReturnNull)` — return null on error instead of throwing
- `.EnableLog((level, ex, msg) => ...)` — console logging via delegate
- `.EnableLogFile("./logs", LogLevel.Debug, async: true)` — file logging
- `.WithLogger(ILogger)` — custom logger
- `.Build()` — create client without connecting
- `.BuildAndRunAsync()` — create + connect + full handshake

**Configuration** (`UaClientOptions`):
- `Timeount` — per-step network timeout in ms (default 30000)
- `SessionTimeout` / `ChannelLifetime` — session/channel lifetime in ms
- `ReconnectMaxRetries` — default -1 (infinite), 0 disables reconnect
- `ReconnectInitialDelayMs` / `ReconnectMaxDelayMs` — exponential backoff params
- `ErrorMode` — Throw (default) or ReturnNull
- `Security.Policy` / `Security.Mode` — security policy and message mode
- `Security.AutoDiscoverServerCertificate` / `AutoAcceptServerCertificate` — auto-discovery and auto-trust
- `Security.UserIdentity.Type` — Anonymous, UserName, Certificate
- `Security.Certificate.AutoGenerate` / `KeySize` / `ValidityYears` — client cert options

### Read

```csharp
// Single node — returns ReadResult { NodeId, StatusCode, DataValue? }
var r = await client.ReadAsync("i=2258");
if (r.StatusCode.IsGood)
    Console.WriteLine($"{r.NodeId}: {r.DataValue?.Value?.Value}");

// Batch — returns ReadResult[]
var results = await client.ReadAsync(new NodeId[] { "i=2256", "i=2258" });
foreach (var r in results)
{
    if (r.StatusCode.IsGood)
        Console.WriteLine($"{r.NodeId}: {r.DataValue?.Value?.Value}");
    else
        Console.WriteLine($"{r.NodeId}: {r.StatusCode.GetStatusText()}");
}

// Typed single-node helper — returns T?
var time = await client.ReadValueAsync<DateTime>("i=2258");
var temp = await client.ReadValueAsync<double>("ns=2;s=Temperature");
```

`ReadResult` properties: `NodeId`, `StatusCode` (non-nullable), `DataValue?`

`ReadValueAsync<T>` returns `T?` directly — casts via `Convert.ChangeType`. Returns `default(T)` on failure or type mismatch.

### Write

```csharp
// Single node — returns WriteResult { NodeId, StatusCode }
var r = await client.WriteAsync("ns=2;s=SetPoint", 42.0);
Console.WriteLine(r.StatusCode.IsGood ? "OK" : r.StatusCode.GetStatusText());

// Batch — returns WriteResult[]
var results = await client.WriteAsync(new WriteValue[]
{
    new() { NodeId = "ns=2;s=Scalar.Double", Value = new DataValue(3.14) },
    new() { NodeId = "ns=2;s=Scalar.Float",  Value = new DataValue(2.718f) },
    new() { NodeId = "ns=2;s=Scalar.Int32",  Value = new DataValue(42) },
});
foreach (var r in results)
    Console.WriteLine($"{r.NodeId}: {(r.StatusCode.IsGood ? "OK" : r.StatusCode.GetStatusText())}");

// Write without checking result
await client.WriteValueAsync<double>("ns=2;s=SetPoint", 42.0);
```

`WriteResult` properties: `NodeId`, `StatusCode` (non-nullable)

`DataValue` constructor: `new DataValue(object)` auto-detects type, or `new DataValue(new Variant(value, VariantType.Float))` for explicit type conversion.

### Browse

```csharp
var results = await client.BrowseAsync("i=85");  // Objects folder
foreach (var desc in results[0].References)
    Console.WriteLine($"{desc.DisplayName?.Text} ({desc.NodeClass}): {desc.NodeId}");

// BrowseNext — continue when ContinuationPoint is present
if (results[0].ContinuationPoint != null)
{
    var next = await client.BrowseNextAsync(results[0].ContinuationPoint);
    foreach (var desc in next[0].References)
        Console.WriteLine($"{desc.DisplayName?.Text}: {desc.NodeId}");
}
```

### Subscribe

```csharp
// Typed single-node subscription
var sub = await client.SubscribeAsync<DateTime>("i=2258", val =>
{
    Console.WriteLine($"ServerTime: {val:HH:mm:ss.fff}");
}, interval: 500);

// Batch subscription — NodeId in callback
var sub = await client.SubscribeAsync(
    new NodeId[] { "i=2258", "ns=2;s=Temperature" },
    (nodeId, value, status) =>
    {
        if (status.IsGood)
            Console.WriteLine($"{nodeId}: {value}");
    },
    interval: 500);

sub.Dispose();  // unsubscribe
```

`DataChangeHandler` delegate: `(NodeId nodeId, object? value, StatusCode status)`
Subscriptions auto-recover on reconnect. Multiple `SubscribeAsync` calls with the same interval share one subscription.

### StatusCode

```csharp
status.IsGood / status.IsBad / status.IsUncertain   // bool properties
status.GetStatusText()  // "Good", "BadNodeIdUnknown", "BadNotWritable", etc.
                        // Falls back to "Bad (0xNNNNNNNN)" for unrecognized codes
```

Predefined status codes (30 total): `StatusCode.Good`, `StatusCode.Bad`, `StatusCode.BadNodeIdUnknown`, `StatusCode.BadAttributeIdInvalid`, `StatusCode.BadUserAccessDenied`, `StatusCode.BadNotWritable`, `StatusCode.BadTypeMismatch`, etc.

### Endpoint Discovery

```csharp
var endpoints = await EndpointDiscoverer.DiscoverAsync("opc.tcp://server:4840");
foreach (var ep in endpoints)
    Console.WriteLine($"{ep.SecurityPolicy} ({ep.SecurityMode}): {ep.EndpointUrl}");
```

`DiscoveredEndpoint` properties: `EndpointUrl`, `SecurityPolicy` (short name), `SecurityPolicyUri`, `SecurityMode`, `SecurityLevel`, `UserTokenTypes`, `IsSecure`

## Security

| Policy | Key Exchange | Symmetric Crypto | Asymmetric Crypto |
|--------|-------------|------------------|-------------------|
| None | — | — | — |
| Basic256Sha256 | RSA-OAEP-SHA256 | AES-256-CBC + HMAC-SHA256 | RSA-SHA256 |
| Aes128Sha256RsaOaep | RSA-OAEP-SHA256 | AES-128-CBC + HMAC-SHA256 | RSA-SHA256 |
| Aes256Sha256RsaPss | RSA-OAEP-SHA256 (PSS) | AES-256-CBC + HMAC-SHA256 | RSA-PSS-SHA256 |

Token types: Anonymous, UserName, X509 Certificate

## Project Structure

| Project | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| TinyUa.Core | Binary encoding, OPC UA types, security policies, logging |
| TinyUa.Transport | Message chunking (OPN/MSG), secure channel, key derivation |
| TinyUa.Client | Fluent API, connection management, services, subscriptions |
| TinyUa.Example | Console self-test app with in-process server |
| TinyUa.Explorer | WPF GUI browser with security endpoint discovery |
| TinyUa.Benchmarks | Performance and crypto primitive benchmarks |
| TinyUa.Client.Tests | xUnit client tests |
| TinyUa.Security.Tests | xUnit security integration tests |
